name: N. J. Latvian
description_short: The New York Latvian Congregation operates two non-profit Latvian schools in New Jersey and Yonkers, offering language education and cultural programming for children Pre-K through 8th grade.
description_long:
The New York Latvian Congregation (Ņujorkas latviešu ev. lut. draudze) is a religious and cultural organization serving the Latvian-American community in the New York metropolitan area. The congregation operates two Latvian schools as part of its mission to preserve Latvian language and culture for younger generations. One school is located in Westfield, New Jersey (in rented space in the New Jersey Parish), and the other is in Yonkers, New York (at the Yonkers Church). Both schools serve as community institutions rather than for-profit educational businesses.

The schools offer comprehensive Latvian language instruction and cultural education for children from Pre-K through 8th grade. The curriculum is set by the American Latvian Association and includes grammar, reading, writing, literature, geography, history, folk dance, and singing. The New Jersey location provides a Latvian-immersion environment where children hear and speak Latvian with peers, plus a play group for younger children. The Yonkers school accepts all children interested in supplementing Latvian language skills and offers Latvian as a Second Language (LSL) classes for adults.

What distinguishes this organization is its dual focus on both heritage preservation and community support. Rather than charging commercial rates, the schools operate as part of a congregation's broader mission. The curriculum is standardized across locations through the American Latvian Association framework, and schools complement academics with cultural events throughout the year. The organization maintains active communication with families through newsletters, Facebook pages, and email subscriptions about congregation activities, events, and camp programs.

This is a niche educational and cultural organization, not a consumer finance company. The website contains no financial products, lending services, loans, credit-related offerings, or consumer finance activity. The current categorization as "payday-alternatives" is entirely inaccurate. This organization provides educational and cultural services to the Latvian-American community as a non-profit religious congregation.
services: Grammar and writing instruction in Latvian
Latvian as a Second Language (LSL) classes for adults
Cultural events and programming throughout the school year
pros: Standardized Latvian curriculum developed by American Latvian Association
Dual-location accessibility (Westfield, NJ and Yonkers, NY)
Serves pre-K through 8th grade with age-appropriate programming
Offers Latvian as a Second Language (LSL) classes for adult learners
Includes cultural activities like folk dance and singing alongside academics
Play group option for children not yet ready for formal schooling
Active community engagement through events, camps, and newsletters
cons: Limited geographic availability (only Westfield, NJ and Yonkers, NY locations)
Website lacks specific pricing, enrollment procedures, or scheduling details
No information about class sizes, teacher qualifications, or accreditation status
Relies on rented space in New Jersey rather than dedicated school facilities
Limited online presence—requires external links to Facebook for current information
best_for: Latvian-American families seeking heritage language education for children
Children from Pre-K to 8th grade in the New York metropolitan area
Adult learners interested in Latvian language instruction as a second language
diagnosis: N. J. Latvian is a non-profit religious congregation offering Latvian language and cultural education, not a consumer finance company. The categorization as a payday-alternative lender is completely inaccurate; this organization should be recategorized as a non-profit educational/cultural institution or removed from the finance database entirely, as it provides no financial services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
